item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Welp, consider me unnerved.

+1, now time to experience some class-1 nightmares

item.title: Re: Staff Post - Open
item.content:encoded: I completely missed this when it was happening, but I am very happy with this crosslink and think Coyote would OF COURSE do that.

item.title: Re: Staff Post - Open
item.content:encoded: The implication of the link was that the DNA would match with SCP-2547-1, who is implied to be the Coyote trickster spirit of Native American myth, not the various coyotes that are humans turned into coyotes and other canines by 2547-1.

item.title: Re: Staff Post - Open
item.content:encoded: The linked SCP says that those coyotes have human genes, so would this mean that anyone who turns into a coyote from the effect of 1579 would have the DNA of a coyote with human DNA?

item.title: Re: Staff Post - Open
item.content:encoded: ye done

item.title: Staff Post - Open
item.content:encoded:
Edits made by author. Timer and crosslink tag removed.

The WikiWalk team is proposing edits to this page. All proposed edits will be in a collapsible below this post, and after 7 days if there are no serious objections to the edits, the feedback received will be taken into account as the edits are made. If you have questions not directly related to the article about this project, please pm a member of the WikiWalk Team

+ Show Block
- Hide Block

Well, gee wiz, a totem pole that infuses people with genetic material and traits from its totems? And one of them is a Coyote? Wouldn't it make sense if that DNA matched SCP-2547-1 who is THE coyote trickster spirit? In all seriousness, I'm quite fond of proposing that link as it not only gives a hint as to who the specific animals from which 1579 gets its powers from are, but that there are at least three other beings similar to 2547-1 out and about in the wild. As such, I am recommending the following edit:

Changing this:

Those of Canis latrans, or the coyote: Subject will grow layered fur like that of an ordinary coyote; additionally, the cartilage in their ears will slough out and be replaced with new cartilage, in a similar shape to that of a coyote. Additionally, the subject's skin will turn black, and their nose will become moist to the touch. Nails are replaced with black canine nails. All DNA samples match that of a particular male coyote.

To this:

Those of Canis latrans, or the coyote: Subject will grow layered fur like that of an ordinary coyote; additionally, the cartilage in their ears will slough out and be replaced with new cartilage, in a similar shape to that of a coyote. Additionally, the subject's skin will turn black, and their nose will become moist to the touch. Nails are replaced with black canine nails. All DNA samples match that of a particular male coyote.

If anyone has other/better examples that could be used here please leave a comment saying so!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: What really broke my suspension of disbelief in this is the fact that ravens have black legs, not yellow. Other than that, it's certainly interesting.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
It's 2016 now! Downvoting1 Crosstesting/crosslinking is no longer discouraged! Go for it!

Footnotes
1. lol, well that was close!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: My understanding was that it only changed DNA in skin. So, instead of biological sex switching, a female test subject would be the same as a male.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I'm honestly not sure. It would change things for sure, but like, the present anatomy would hardly just replace itself, as that's stuff created during gestation, rather than later on. For most cells it'd hardly be a change as they already only have one copy of X chromosome active - it's where Barr bodies come from.
I guess, best check with an actual biologist.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I don't think that's how that works. I think if her DNA became male, she'd just have her physical gender changed.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
It says that all the new DNA is form all male animals
so surely if a woman touches it, will she become a bisexual?

item.title: Re: Very Good
item.content:encoded:
Containment procedures don't need to be detailed, especially to the point of adding in protocols that aren't essential to the surety of containment. If the chain linked fence isn't imperatively necessary, then there's no need to include it.

Consider this guide: http://www.scp-wiki.net/mackenzie-containment

Edit: For the record, this is my opinion. Some might prefer more detailed containment procedures, but for the purpose of a narrative, I'd prefer a locker if that's all that's needed, as opposed to a triple-locked strongbox kept in a square room with a combination lock of seven digits also thumbprint scanner and voice recognition etc.

item.title: Very Good
item.content:encoded: I thought this was good, the idea of the totem poll was creepy, in a subtle way. My only complaint would be that, you don't go into enough detail on containment procedures. It doesn't have to be much, just say that it is being contained by a chained linked fence.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Instead of double-posting, edit your previous post using the "edit" function under the "options" tab to the lower right of every comment. That prevents spam buildup, and it's in the rules. :)

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Severe, not sever.
LOL, typo

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I would like to know:
Does the transformation hurt?
What kinds of sever psychological effect does it have on subjects? Some cases, maybe?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Now I'm curious.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: heisrightyouknow.jpg

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Great idea, great flavor. I can totally imagine this being a Native American artifact, used for their rituals. It just makes a lot of sense and is not random or farfetched in any way.

Also, I feel that this

SCP-1579 did not feature on the path previous to this initial encounter.

doesn't appear to be really needed.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I really like this a lot. It's one of those scips that make sense. Turning people into regional animals is a logical effect for a native North American totem pole, and it conjures a really cool picture of raven-men dancing around it, chanting to their crazy forest gods.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: The fact that you think this is kinky says a lot more about you than it does about the article.

item.title: And besides, i could write the best furry article if i wanted to
item.content:encoded:
Tip:

Written to buck a trend of furry kink articles at the time
Entire article is play on the word (and concept of a) "Skinwalker"
Bad endings are best endings

Point:

Missed

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Kinks:

Furries
Transformation Play
Bad Endings

Vote:

Down

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: The funny thing is, unless I zoom in your avatar looks just like my friends avatar, of him in his fursuit. :p

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
A) If you have enough layers of skin peel off, you're probably getting quite deep into the body. In fact you'll hit bone, and vital organs, etc. The final result could well be a flayed corpse.

B) Hating on furries is both stupid and irrelevant. You might as well look at bugs bunny and go "Oh NOES, TEH FURRIES!".
Seriously, get over it.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: The DNA is only being found in the skin. It's possible to have more than one set of DNA in your body (although, if it's from another species there has to be something special going on to prevent you rejecting it). Think of it like a skin transplant.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: [Never mind.]

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I don't agree with the 2nd law of biology. There was life before the first cell… just not as we know it now. Protocellular Biogenesis closely following Abiogenesis.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Biology doesn't even have any laws to break.

Not true.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Biology doesn't even have any laws to break.

Come to think of it, god knows what resuts from long term exposure.

EDIT: That is to say, Dexanote probably knows.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
DNA that was a match wouldn't provide bipedalism or many humanoid qualities.

Or it would, because SCPs frequently laugh in the face of everything that we know.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I was going to say basically the same thing about that sentence, but mention "shudder" too. I think "vibrate" sounds more clinical.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Well if SCPs can break the laws of physics then they can do the same to biology as well.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Fixed!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Hnnnnng I want to cross-test with the output of my [Venus Statue] so badly right now, but I'd probably get run out of the Foundation…

Also, we now have something good that turns humans into hybrids, and something (apparently) good that turns animals into hybrids; I think we can call it a day and hope nothing else pops up.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I might suggest:

The specific transformation induced will cycle in the order listed above, regardless whether a new subject activates SCP-1579 or it is a repeated activation by the same subject.

Take that, clean it up to be good English, you get the gist.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: It's nice to know how you really feel, I guess.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: The first one. I'm not quite sure how to word that more clearly; i'm happy to take any suggestions.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
One thing I don't quite understand - you say that transformations occur in a cycle - does that mean that if 4 D-class touch it, the first one will turn into a raven, the second into a bear, etc.?

Or will they all turn into a raven, and the bear transformation will occur only if a raven touches it a second time?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: • Those of Octopus paxarbolis…

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I like it. Particularly the choice of animals (the frog is the neatest, as it's the most unusual).

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I am now greatly a-feared.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Not sure why the person in the addendum died from bloodloss. I don't want to like this because it turns people into furries, but I do definitely like the idea of the skin peeling away to reveal different skin underneath. Having it be directly related to the animals carved in the totem pole (despite them not all being there; that some of them are is enough) seems kind of hokey though.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
When physically touched by a human being, SCP-1579 will shudder slightly, motivated by an unknown force.

"Struck", "moved", "caused" or "induced" might be a better word choice than "motivated". "Motivated", while technically correct, sounds kind of weird when used like that.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
It's one of those things that sounds kinda cool if you don't think about it…

…then kinda horrifying if you do. I do not want to be Jo-Jo the Dog-Faced Boy, no sir, not even if it cut down on my heating bills.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Way to stereotype.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I was tempted, but lemurs do not live in the Pacific northwest. Sillybilly.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: • Those of Lepilemur mustelinus, or lemurs…

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I apologize for not being able to provide any specific criticism, but this doesn't do it for me and I'm not sure why. Downvoted.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Why did it end up on that trail? Bum bum BUM!
Upvoted-great job!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I don't take it personally. Appreciate your input.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: … Bright, honestly, this makes me feel the same as when Gears upvoted 1479. Thanks.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I like it, reminds me of those Native American legends where the spirit gods 'bless' tribesmen.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This doesn't make furries, it makes miserable freaks.

Edit: point taken.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I sort of dislike it because of the DNA testing logs. DNA that was a match wouldn't provide bipedalism or many humanoid qualities.

I like most of your work though, so I figure this is just an off thing.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I like it dex. I really like it. To the point that this may end up causing plot in Tamlin House.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Really, if they honestly believe that I was trying to make a self-insert furry fandom SCP, they can downvote if they want. I have no problems with the fandom, but it's not my thing.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Be prepared for the "It makes furries!!" downvotes…

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I kept seeing animal transformation SCPs die in droves. Wanted to see if i could pull one off. Thought a skinwalker angle was the best way to take it.
